title: Proclamation No. 616, s. 1969
text:
MALACAÑANG RESIDENCE OF THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 616

EXCLUDING FROM THE OPERATION OF PROCLAMATION NO. 42, DATED JULY 5, 1954, AS AMENDED BY PROCLAMATION NO. 481, DATED OCTOBER 24, 1968, WHICH ESTABLISHED THE QUEZON MEMORIAL PARK; SITUATED IN DILIMAN, QUEZON CITY, A CERTAIN PARCEL OF THE LAND EMBRACED THEREIN AND RESERVING THE SAME FOR THE PHILIPPINE SCIENCE HIGH SCHOOL.

By virtue of the powers vested in me by law, I, FERDINAND E. MARCOS, President of the Philippines, do hereby exclude from the operation of Proclamation No. 42, dated July 5, 1954, as amended by Proclamation No. 481, dated October 24, 1968, which, established the Quezon Memorial Park, situated at Diliman, Quezon City, certain parcel of land embraced therein and reserve the same for the Philippine Science High School Site, subject to private rights, if any there be, and to future survey, under the administration of the Philippine Science High School, and were particularly described as follows:

A parcel of land (Lot RP 3-B-4-B-L-B), situated in the District of Diliman, Quezon City. Bounded on the North along line 8 to 9 by Lot R.P. 3-B-4-B-1A, on the East along lines 9 to 10 by Lot R.P. 3-B4-B-1-A, on the South, along line 10 to 1 by Lot R.P. 3-B-4-B-1-A; and on the West, along lines 1-2-3-4-5-6-7-8 by Lot R.P. 3-B-,2-1-A. Beginning at a point marked “1” on plan, being N. deg. 15 min.=E., 982.32 meters from B.L.L.M. No. 7, Quezon City.

beginning containing an area of one hundred twenty-seven thousand (127,000) square meters, more or less. All points referred to are indicated and are marked on the ground by Steel Bars 2 x 100 cms. Bearings true; date of original survey Dec. 1910 to June 1911 and that of the segregation survey May 21, 23 and May 26-27, 1969.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Republic of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done in the City of Manila, this 6th day of October, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and sixty-nine.

(Sgd.) FERDINAND E. MARCOS President of the Philippines

By the President:

(Sgd.) ERNESTO M. MACEDA Executive Secretary

Source: National Library
